explanations.1: This line tells make to include variables from a .env file if it exists, so you can set configuration values outside this file.
explanations.4: The $$ is an escape sequence in make that becomes a single $ when executed; it's getting your current user and group IDs to pass to Docker so containers run with your permissions.
explanations.17: The \033 sequences are ANSI color codes that make the text yellow and bold in your terminal; the ${WEB_PORT} gets replaced with the actual port number from your environment.
explanations.52: The -@ prefix means make will ignore errors if this command fails and won't print the command itself, so a missing directory won't stop the build.
explanations.53: The $$ escapes the $ for make, and --detach means Docker will run in the background instead of taking over your terminal.
